'''David Atkins''' is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played for [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] in the [[NRL]] and for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
Atkins made his first grade debut for Canberra in round 9 of the [[1997 Super League (Australia) season|1997 Super League season]] against the [[Perth Reds]] at [[Bruce Stadium]]. Atkins was later recalled into the Canberra team for the 1997 finals series and played in two finals matches against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] and [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla]]. In the [[1998 NRL season]], Atkins played eight games for Canberra including their elimination final victory over [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]]. Atkins last game for Canberra came in the [[2000 NRL season]] where the club lost 38–10 against the [[Sydney Roosters]] in that years elimination final. In 2001, Atkins joined English side Huddersfield where he played a total of 31 matches over two seasons.<ref>Alan Whiticker/Glen Hudson: The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players. (1995 edition) {{ISBN|1875169571}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/about/all-time-first-grade-player-list/|title=Canberra Raiders all time first grade player list|website=www.raiders.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/story/6130914/woden-valley-start-up-the-green-machine/|title=Woden Valley Rams coach Lincoln Withers calls on ex-Canberra Raiders|website=www.canberratimes.com.au}}</ref>
